summary: Bilateral temporal-mandibular joint (TMJ) ankylosis causes obstructive sleep apnea by restricting mandibular motion, leading to postero-displacement of the tongue and subsequent airway collapse. This is a direct mechanical mechanism involving lower facial structure rather than nasopharyngeal obstructions.
